replication_study.hypothesis: Participants assigned to the two-stage tournament (treat = 2) will exert higher first-stage effort (effort_t1) than participants assigned to the strategically equivalent one-stage tournament (treat = 1).

data_plan.dataset_identifier: FinalData.csv (cleaned dataset contained in replication_data folder)
data_plan.source_type: Laboratory experiment decision records (z-Tree export cleaned to CSV)
data_plan.wave_or_subset: All sessions conducted between 2 Nov 2021 and 30 Nov 2021; includes both OS and TS treatments only (excludes OSL).
data_plan.access_details: Openly provided in /app/data/original/28/input/replication_data; no additional restrictions.

qualification.explanation: The dataset is a completely separate set of laboratory sessions that follow the identical experimental protocol of the original study. Using new independent sessions tests whether the focal behavioral difference is reproducible.
qualification.similarity_to_original: Same z-Tree program and variable names (see data cleaning and analysis.do) and same treatment definitions (treat=1 OS; treat=2 TS) reported in the original paper (original_paper.pdf, Method section pp. 7–9). Outcome variable effort_t1 corresponds to the first-stage effort used for the original claim (post_registration.json, results.summary).
qualification.deviation_from_original: Original experiment recruited 320 subjects (post_registration.json, data.sample_size); the replication dataset contains 312 observations collected approximately ten years later and therefore represents a new sample. The replication data were collected at different calendar dates (sessioncode variable, FinalData.csv) and are not included in the original analysis.

data_plan.notes: File encoding already UTF-8. Variable treat is numeric (1/2). No second-stage winners’ wage variables are required for the focal test.

planned_method.steps:
1. Load FinalData.csv from /app/data.
2. Verify presence of variables treat and effort_t1.
3. Split data into OS (treat==1) and TS (treat==2) groups.
4. Compute group means, medians, and sample sizes for effort_t1.
5. Conduct two-sided Mann-Whitney U-test comparing effort_t1 between TS and OS.
6. Save summary statistics and test results to replication_results.json.
planned_method.models: Non-parametric comparison of independent samples (Mann-Whitney U). Descriptive means/medians also reported.
planned_method.control_variables: None for focal test (mirrors original Mann-Whitney design).

planned_estimation_and_test.estimation: Difference in central tendency of effort_t1 between treatments (U statistic)

planned_method.missing_data_handling: List-wise deletion (rows with NA in treat or effort_t1 are dropped; none expected).
planned_method.inference_criteria: Reject null if p < 0.05 (two-sided). Direction expected: TS > OS.

files.replication_analysis.py: Loads FinalData.csv, performs descriptive statistics and Mann-Whitney U-test, prints results, and saves them as replication_results.json inside /app/data.

codebase.notes: The script autodetects FinalData.csv under /app/data via recursive search, so no hard-coded paths are required.

analysis.comparison_metrics:
1) Mean and median effort_t1 for each treatment.
2) Mann-Whitney U statistic and p-value. Compare direction and significance level to original (original_paper: z=2.536, p=0.011).
